personas.description: Algorithmic or systematic crypto traders who run delta-neutral or basis-trade strategies across CEXs. They actively monitor funding rates to time entries into long-spot / short-perp positions and need real-time Z-score alerts to act within the 8-hour funding window.
personas.primary_goal: Capture funding rate mean-reversion and cross-exchange arbitrage alpha with minimal manual monitoring
pain_points: Must poll 3+ exchange APIs separately and normalize data formats
Historical funding rate data is fragmented; computing rolling Z-scores requires custom pipelines
Short-lived arbitrage windows close before manual detection
No single dashboard aggregates funding rate anomalies across Binance, OKX, and Bybit

personas.description: Professional or semi-professional market makers who provide liquidity on both CEX perpetuals and DeFi perpetual protocols (e.g., dYdX, GMX). They hedge inventory risk using funding rate signals and need early warning when CEX funding diverges significantly from DeFi rates.
personas.primary_goal: Optimize hedging costs by shifting positions to the exchange offering the most favorable funding rate
pain_points: Funding rate divergences between CEX and DeFi are hard to track in one view
Existing tools focus on single-exchange dashboards without cross-exchange spread alerts
Latency in detecting spread opportunities leads to missed profit windows
Need programmatic JSON output to feed into existing execution bots

personas.description: Research analysts at crypto-native hedge funds who use funding rate regimes as a macro sentiment indicator. They integrate funding rate Z-scores into broader quantitative models for position sizing and risk management rather than direct execution.
personas.primary_goal: Generate reliable funding rate regime signals for inclusion in multi-factor alpha models and risk reports
pain_points: Building and maintaining data pipelines for funding rate history across exchanges is overhead for a research team
Inconsistent data quality and staleness across exchange APIs
Lack of standardized Z-score methodology makes cross-team collaboration harder
Need structured JSON output that integrates cleanly into existing data infrastructure (BigQuery, Pandas, etc.)

use_cases.trigger: Funding rate Z-score (30-day rolling) for BTC or ETH on any monitored exchange exceeds |2|
use_cases.frequency: Approximately 2-4 times per month per asset, based on historical BTC/ETH funding rate volatility cycles

use_cases.trigger: Funding rate spread between any two exchanges (Binance, OKX, Bybit) exceeds 0.05% for the same asset
use_cases.frequency: Approximately 1-3 times per month; more frequent during high-volatility regimes (e.g., post-FOMC, post-halving)

use_cases.scenario: Funding rate regime monitoring for risk management
use_cases.trigger: Scheduled 8-hour data collection cycle completes and outputs structured JSON with current rates, Z-scores, and any active alerts
use_cases.frequency: 3 times per day (every 8 hours, aligned with standard funding rate settlement periods)

market_size_estimate.total_addressable: Approximately 200,000-300,000 active crypto derivatives traders globally who trade perpetual contracts on Binance, OKX, and Bybit. This includes retail quant traders, professional prop desks, and institutional funds with crypto exposure.
market_size_estimate.serviceable: Approximately 15,000-25,000 traders and researchers who actively use quantitative tools (APIs, bots, custom dashboards) for funding rate analysis. This excludes purely manual traders who rely on exchange-native UIs.

competitive_landscape.name: Coinglass Funding Rate Dashboard
competitive_landscape.overlap: High overlap on data visualization -- Coinglass shows real-time funding rates across major exchanges with historical charts
competitive_landscape.differentiation: This Skill provides automated Z-score-based anomaly detection and structured JSON alerts rather than a passive dashboard. Coinglass requires manual monitoring; this Skill pushes actionable signals.

competitive_landscape.name: Laevitas Analytics
competitive_landscape.overlap: Moderate overlap -- Laevitas offers derivatives analytics including funding rate heatmaps and cross-exchange comparisons
competitive_landscape.differentiation: Laevitas is a premium SaaS product aimed at institutional users. This Skill is lightweight, self-hosted, free (public API only), and outputs machine-readable JSON for direct integration into trading bots.

competitive_landscape.name: Custom exchange API scripts (in-house)
competitive_landscape.overlap: Full overlap in data sourcing -- many quant teams build their own funding rate collection scripts
competitive_landscape.differentiation: This Skill provides a standardized, pre-built solution with Z-score logic, entry/exit conditions, and staleness checks already implemented, reducing development and maintenance overhead for small teams.

competitive_landscape.name: Velo Data
competitive_landscape.overlap: Moderate overlap -- Velo provides derivatives data feeds including funding rates with historical lookback
competitive_landscape.differentiation: Velo Data is a paid data provider focused on raw data delivery. This Skill adds an analytical layer (Z-score regime detection, spread thresholds) and produces actionable alerts rather than raw feeds.
